texto: Try not to compromise. So many people don’t do what they really want in their hearts because they feel like they’re not good enough, or they’re not smart enough, or they’re not talented enough… anything. And that doesn’t matter. In order for you to live a remarkable life — in order for you to live a life that is fulfilling — you need to be able to go after what you want. And if you don’t, you’re not going to achieve it — ever.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Debbie Millman: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “Try not to compromise. So many people don’t do what they really want in their hearts because they…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, coragem, enough pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de reflexao.

frase_original: Try not to compromise. So many people don’t do what they really want in their hearts because they feel like they’re not good enough, or they’re not smart enough, or they’re not talented enough… anything. And that doesn’t matter. In order for you to live a remarkable life — in order for you to live a life that is fulfilling — you need to be able to go after what you want. And if you don’t, you’re not going to achieve it — ever.
